report_name: Warning: Free VPN Browser Extensions Caught Harvesting Clipboard Data

attack_patterns.name: Software Extensions
attack_patterns.description: Malicious browser extensions for Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox, masquerading as free VPN services, were used to harvest user data.

attack_patterns.name: Clipboard Data
attack_patterns.description: The malicious extensions continuously monitor and exfiltrate the contents of the user's clipboard, stealing sensitive information like passwords, credit card numbers, and cryptocurrency wallet keys.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Captured clipboard data is sent to an attacker-controlled remote server, often over HTTPS to blend in with normal web traffic.

malware.name: Malicious VPN Browser Extension
malware.description: A class of malicious browser extensions for Chrome and Firefox that masquerade as free VPN services to secretly harvest and exfiltrate user clipboard data.

infrastructure.name: Data Exfiltration Server
infrastructure.description: A remote server controlled by the attacker that receives clipboard data exfiltrated by the malicious VPN browser extensions.

indicators.name: Suspicious Browser Extension Installation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Browser Extension Installation
id: 2b9c1f8a-4d7e-4b0f-8c9a-1f2e3d4b5a6b
status: experimental
description: Detects file creation events in common browser extension directories, which could indicate the installation of a new, potentially malicious, extension.
references:
  - https://www.manilatimes.net/2026/07/20/tmt-newswire/globenewswire/free-vpn-extensions-caught-harvesting-clipboard-data-cyberinsider-warns-browser-users/2387620
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1176
level: low
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Extensions\'
      - '\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\manifest.json'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the installation of new browser extensions in user profiles for Chrome and Firefox by monitoring for the creation of manifest.json files. This could indicate the installation of a potentially malicious extension as described in the article.
